books.title: The Secret Tomb
books.author: Maurice Leblanc
books.summary: A young travelling circus owner discovers a clue to ancestral treasure.

books.title: Running a Thousand Miles for Freedom
books.author: William Craft
books.summary: The authors escape from slavery by posing as a slaveowner and his slave.

books.title: A Yankee in the Trenches
books.author: Robert Derby Holmes
books.summary: An American describes his experiences in the British Army in the First World War.

books.title: Eminent Victorians
books.author: Lytton Strachey
books.summary: Four witty and biting biographies of Victorians that take aim at the moral superiority of their subject’s time.

books.title: Five Children and It
books.author: E. Nesbit
books.summary: Five children encounter an ancient magical creature who grants them wishes—all of which go comically wrong.

books.title: Our American Cousin
books.author: Tom Taylor
books.summary: A rough American from a distant branch of the Trenchard family returns to England and quickly becomes embroiled in the family’s affairs.

books.title: The Trachiniae
books.author: Sophocles
books.summary: A woman unintentionally kills her demigod husband with a poisoned robe in a desperate attempt to reclaim his love.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: Clark Ashton Smith
books.summary: A collection of short fiction by Clark Ashton Smith, ordered by date of publication.

books.title: Our Mutual Friend
books.author: Charles Dickens
books.summary: When the body of an unknown man is dragged from the river Thames, a series of complications involving the will of a wealthy man entangle the lives of several Londoners.

books.title: Melmoth the Wanderer
books.author: Charles Robert Maturin
books.summary: A set of overlapping tales tell the story of a mysterious, malevolent, ageless stranger.

books.title: The Mirror of the Sea
books.author: Joseph Conrad
books.summary: An autobiographical memoir on Joseph Conrad’s maritime experiences and his relationship with the sea.

books.title: The D’Arblay Mystery
books.author: R. Austin Freeman
books.summary: A doctor finds himself entangled in the mysterious death of an artist after discovering his body in a secluded pond.

books.title: Journals
books.author: Alexander Mackenzie
books.summary: Explorer Alexander Mackenzie details his journeys to both the Arctic and Pacific Oceans in his personal journals.

books.title: Moribund Society and Anarchy
books.author: Jean Grave
books.summary: Jean Grave systemically critiques bourgeois society from an anarchist perspective.

books.title: Victory Odes
books.author: Pindar
books.summary: A collection of lyric poems celebrating the triumphs of ancient Greek athletes in various sporting events.

books.title: The Indiscreet Jewels
books.author: Denis Diderot
books.summary: The Sultan of the Congo brings about philosophical inquiry and scandal when he is granted a magical ring that permits him to compel speech from the vaginas of the women of his court.

books.title: The Island of Doctor Moreau
books.author: H. G. Wells
books.summary: A shipwrecked man is rescued by a boat and left on a strange island owned by a physiologist who experiments in vivisection.

books.title: Lord Arthur Savile’s Crime and Other Stories
books.author: Oscar Wilde
books.summary: A collection of Oscar Wilde’s short mysteries.

books.title: A Day at a Time
books.author: Archibald Alexander
books.summary: A 1916 Christian devotional reflecting on faith and morality during World War I.

books.title: My Four Weeks in France
books.author: Ring Lardner
books.summary: A sports columnist and humorist travels to France as a war correspondent during World War I and recounts his experiences on the assignment.
